Turn 76 ‚ÄĒ final appendix freeze: D.1/D.2 pivot contracts, no new obstruction layer I re-read the three attachments for this turn only. The problem is still the over-G isomorphism question for őďS,őďH, i.e. whether there is a continuous isomorphism commuting with the canonical projections to G.The attached history has already frozen the structural proof: residual induction, coefficient Koszul‚ÄďWitt induction, nonlinear Certificates D.1‚ÄďD.4, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness produce an over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF with ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH, hence őďS‚ČÖGőďH.The useful Turn 76 move is to finish the appendix contract for D.1 and D.2 in the same notation-safe style Turn 75 imposed. No new Magnus degree, coefficient quotient, or subcase should be opened.

1. Global notation firewall

All relation rows are right őõ=Z2 [[G]]-rows. Do not print the key 2√ó2 maps as naked arrays unless the row convention is explicitly stated.

Use row operators as primary notation:U0 (a,b)=(a(s‚ąí1)+b(s‚ąí2),¬†a+2b).ThenU0 (s+3,‚ąí2)=(s2+1,s‚ąí1),and the determinant is 2(s‚ąí1)‚ąí(s‚ąí2)=s,a unit. This is the dangerous Ef-piece of the global H1 -Nielsen matrix carryingwS=(ŌÉ+3E,‚ąí2)towH=(Eg0+3E‚ąí2,ŌÉ‚ąíE).

For coefficient normalization, use the row operator N(a,b)=(a‚ąí(s+1)b,¬†b).

Since(s2+1)‚ąí(s+1)(s‚ąí1)=2,we getN(s2+1,s‚ąí1)=(2,t),t=s‚ąí1.This is the exact normalization feeding the Koszul‚ÄďWitt coefficient induction; after it, the coefficient row is the regular row (2,t), not the raw (2+2t+t2,t).

2. D.1 should be a stack-rank verifier contract Certificate D.1 should not be presented as six ad hoc witness rows in the main appendix. The structural certificate is the full-rank direct-sum mapS10:L8‚äēL8‚ü∂L9‚äēL5[2],S10 (u,v)=(‚ąā8 (u,v),¬†B10 (u,v)),where‚ąā8 (u,v)=[A,u]+[B,v].

The verifier target is rank(‚ąā8)=56,rank(S10)=62.

SincedimL9=56,dimL5[2]=6,the rank jump from 56 to 62 provesB10 (ker‚ąā8)=L5[2].This is exactly the invariant form of the residual degree-10 Bockstein witness package. It closes the first genuine even restricted-square obstruction without depending on a particular displayed witness basis. The earlier explicit witnesses may remain in a supplementary table, but the main certificate should be the stack-rank statement.

3. D.2 should be a scalar transgression verifier contract Certificate D.2 should likewise avoid a giant coordinate dump. Its invariant content is the lower-base transgression of the degree-16 missing functional.

Use the lower-base wordő∑4=Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4))).The D.2 verifier contract is four finite checks:S(Y0(8)ő∑4,¬†Y1(8),¬†q(8))‚Č°X2 (modI9).Letőė16:K‚Č§15‚ÜíL8[2]be the degree-16 high-torsor square map. Verify rank(őė16)=35,imőė16=kerőĽ,where őĽ=coeff ((AB+BA)4)[2]+coeff(B8)[2].

Then verify the two scalar valuesőĽ(ő©16fix)=1,őĽ(ő©16ő∑)=0.This says: the fixed high gauge has the codimension-one obstruction, but the lower-base deformation ő∑4 moves the class into the high image kerőĽ. Therefore the degree-16 obstruction is not invariant. This is the structural pattern the sweep repeatedly found: fixed-gauge tails are misleading; full lower-plus-high torsors are the invariant object.

4. D.3 and D.4 stay as already frozen D.3 is already executable in stack-rank form. DefineV=3L5‚äē3L6‚äē3L7andS4:V‚ÜíT6‚äēT7‚äēT8‚äēF2,S4 (z)=(D5,6,7 z,¬†őľ4B4 (z)).

The certificate is rank(D5,6,7)=60,rank(S4)=61.

Since H4(2)is one-dimensional, this rank jump is exactly the assertion that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed őľ4=1 scalar to 0.D.4 remains the one-line quotient witness:[B,B8]=0,so(0,B8)‚ąąker([A,‚ąí]+[B,‚ąí]),andőł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2].

BecauseőĽ((B8)[2])=1,we have kerőĽ+imőł80=L8[2].Thus the residual d=8 codimension-one miss disappears after quotienting by H8(2)=L8[2]/imőł80.

5. Final frozen appendix dependency graph

The manuscript appendix should now use this exact sequence:

Orientation¬†ŌĀS=rS‚ąí1.Right-row¬†H1

Nielsen¬†operator¬†U0.Coefficient¬†row¬†normalization¬†N:(s2+1,s‚ąí1)‚Ü¶(2,t).Primitive-boundary¬†verifier¬†for¬†Ln,‚ąād,őłd0.Residual¬†induction¬†Cm=Lm.Certificate¬†D:¬†S10,ő∑4,S4,őł80(0,B8).Normalized¬†Koszul‚ÄďWitt¬†coefficient¬†induction.Finite¬†filtered¬†Nielsen¬†compactness.For the final compactness step, use the corrected finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle sets SN,J, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms. The finite data are taken overPN,J=P‚ą©(1+IN+JM),with first layer UmodJ, cocycle entries satisfying the finite relation induced by ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2, and twisted substitution sendingŌĀS=rS‚ąí1‚Ü¶rH (modPN,J).

The certificates prove SN,JÓÄ†=‚ąÖ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation. Compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit point, hence a continuous over-G automorphism ő¶:F=P‚čäG‚ÜíFwithő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH.

Therefore ő¶(‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F)=‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F,and passing to quotients givesőďS‚ČÖGőďH.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 76 freezes the final appendix in verifier-contract form. D.1 is the stack-rank map S10 with ranks 56,62; D.2 is the scalar lower-base transgression contract for ő∑4; D.3 is the stack-rank map S4 with ranks 60,61; and D.4 is the quotient witness őł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]with őĽ=1. With the row-operator convention for U0 and N, the certificate proof yields a continuous over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF satisfying ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH, hence őďS‚ČÖGőďH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining work is external reproducibility/presentation: in a standalone paper, attach the actual D.1 and D.2 verifier scripts or pivot tables realizing the rank/scalar contracts above.

NEXT: Write the final manuscript appendix exactly in this frozen form: row-operator convention first, then Certificates 0‚ÄďF, with D.1/D.2 stated as verifier contracts and D.3/D.4 as already executable/minimal witnesses, ending with finite filtered Nielsen compactness and the descent to őďS‚ČÖGőďH.[TURN76-FINAL-APPENDIX-FROZEN-VERIFIER-CONTRACTS]
